title: Token
content:
- ### OntologyBlock
- ## About ERC-3643 Standard
  - Includes fungible tokens (interchangeable units), non-fungible tokens (unique items), and hybrid multi-token standards.
  - ERC-20 tokens, stablecoins, governance tokens, utility tokens
  - Digital art, CryptoKitties, collectibles, real estate tokens, in-game items
  - Event tickets (fungible until used, then non-fungible as proof of attendance), gaming items with limited quantities
  - Filecoin (storage access), Basic Attention Token (ad platform), Chainlink (oracle services)
  - Tokenized company shares, real estate tokens, tokenized bonds
  - UNI (Uniswap), AAVE (Aave), MKR (MakerDAO)
  - USDC (fiat-backed), DAI (crypto-collateralized), USDT (Tether)
  - BTC (Bitcoin), ETH (Ethereum), SOL (Solana), ADA (Cardano)
  - USDC, LINK, UNI, DAI
  - CryptoKitties, Bored Ape Yacht Club, ENS domains
  - Enjin gaming items, The Sandbox assets
  - Tokenized securities, regulated asset tokens
  - Ethereum (post-Merge issuance), Polkadot, Cosmos
  - Ethereum (net deflationary), BNB (quarterly burns)
  - Bitcoin (21M cap), Litecoin (84M cap)
  - Bitcoin halving every 210,000 blocks (~4 years), Litecoin halving
  - Bitcoin's 21 million BTC cap
  - EIP-1559 base fee burning, BNB quarterly burns, buyback-and-burn programs
  - Popularized by CryptoKitties (2017) and subsequent NFT art boom (2021).
  - Proposed by Fabian Vogelsteller in 2015 as EIP-20.
  - Proposed by William Entriken, Dieter Shirley, Jacob Evans, Nastassia Sachs in January 2018.

ontology.definition: Permissioned token standard with on-chain identity verification for regulatory compliance.
